refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combines multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To be atomic and solution-neutral, it is necessary to separate these into distinct FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite analysis without implying any implementation details.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and present an analysis specifically of the terrain characteristics of the worksite, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain.
The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of obstacles within the worksite area.
The NGHE solution shall provide representation of the work-zone within the worksite area.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features
Extract and classify terrain features relevant to safety and operations
Create a geospatial map integrating terrain data with safety and operational policies
Provide an interface to access and update the terrain model for planning and execution
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features

potential_sources: Integration of terrain data with safety and operational policies may introduce coupling if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: Selecting a DP focused on generating a digital terrain model keeps the solution modular and allows separate DPs or modules to handle policy integration and feature classification, supporting independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data sources
Risk that terrain model may not capture all features required by safety and operational policies
Assumes that subsequent modules will handle policy integration and feature classification
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider decomposing the FR further to explicitly separate terrain modeling from policy integration and feature classification to enhance modularity and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses data processing and environment modeling functions, including generation of digital terrain models from sensor data.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor and positional data necessary for terrain modeling.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands to initiate or update terrain model generation tasks.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed terrain model data to other system components or external users.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain modeling is a core data processing function; minimal diffusion as data inputs and outputs are well encapsulated.
Low containment since vehicle focuses on mobility and control; terrain modeling would require extensive data sharing and cause diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control and user interface; terrain modeling would be peripheral and cause high diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; terrain modeling is unrelated and would cause significant diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing the digital terrain model generation DP, as it naturally contains the data processing and environment modeling functions with minimal diffusion outside the module.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may affect model accuracy.
Integration with other modules may require well-defined interfaces to avoid data duplication or inconsistency.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing key site features' directly satisfies the FR 'The NGHE solution shall provide analysis of the worksite terrain' by producing a digital representation of the terrain, which is a clear and measurable functional requirement.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data input which may affect model accuracy.
Integration with other modules may require well-defined interfaces to avoid data duplication or inconsistency.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and minimal diffusion risk as it encapsulates terrain modeling functions and data processing. Other modules show low containment and high diffusion scores, indicating that the DP is well-contained within the NGHE Solution IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through the availability of the generated worksite model/map to planning and execution functions, as specified in the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM boundary is clear and appropriate, given the module's scope and containment scores.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justifications and no recommended revisions. The DP fully satisfies the FR, the IM boundary is well-contained with minimal diffusion risks, and verification is feasible. Although there are some diffusion risks related to telemetry data and module integration, these are acknowledged and managed within the current IM boundary. Given the high safety criticality, the current decomposition is sufficiently detailed and stable to proceed without further sub-FR derivation at this time.

assumptions: Telemetry data input will be timely and accurate enough to maintain model accuracy.
Interfaces between modules will be well-defined to prevent data duplication or inconsistency.
open_risks: Dependency on telemetry data accuracy and timeliness.
Potential integration challenges with other modules requiring strict interface definitions.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the generated digital terrain model meets acceptance criteria.
Interface specifications and integration test results demonstrating minimal data inconsistency or duplication.
